item.title: Snow Forecast (2026/02/01)
item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:

Snow Forecast delivers the latest snow conditions and forecasts directly from the slopes of ski resorts across Europe and Central Asia. (Broadcast 2026/02/01)

item.title: Does Donald Trump have a coherent Iran strategy? | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Iran's top security official says there's been progress towards re-opening negotiations with the United States. As the US military build-up in the region gathers momentum, President Trump's intentions towards Iran are far from clear. Trump says he wants to prevent the regime from developing nuclear weapons, and an end to the crackdown on anti-government protests. But he also appears to be softening his tone on military action.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1
#Trump #Iran

item.title: EV sales in the US plummet | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Clean vehicle subsidies in the US expired in September 2025. The results are in and they’re not looking good for electric vehicles.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Over 200 killed in mine collapse in eastern Congo
item.author: Channel 4 News
item.content:encoded: At least 200 people are said to have been killed in eastern Congo – after a landslide earlier this week caused several mines to collapse. Officials said some people could still be trapped in the debris at the site in Rubaya – which lies in an area controlled by M23 rebels.

item.title: Gaza: Israeli strikes kill at least 30, hospital officials say
item.author: Ciaran Jenkins
item.content:encoded: Hospital officials in Gaza say at least 30 people have been killed in a wave of Israeli airstrikes across the territory. Israel said it had targeted Hamas and Islamic Jihad commanders – claiming they had violated the ceasefire. Gaza officials said at least 6 children were among the dead.

item.title: ‘Confident Epstein and Maxwell are not the only sexual abusers’, says survivors’ lawyer
item.author: Ciaran Jenkins
item.content:encoded: We spoke to Gloria Allred – who’s represented some of Epstein’s victims.Yesterday US deputy attorney general Todd Blanche said he didn’t think any further men would be uncovered in the files other than Epstein. We asked her if she agreed.

item.title: Multiple coordinated attacks shock Pakistan | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Eleven civilians, 10 security personnel and 67 militants were killed in multiple attacks across Pakistan's Balochistan province, authorities say. The separatist group, Baloch Liberation Army, claimed responsibility; BLA videos show women fighters taking part in the attacks.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Russia, Ukraine halt energy attacks during cold spell | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Russia and Ukraine say they've halted strikes on each other's energy infrastructure after a request from the White House. Ukrainian President Volodymr Zelenksyy says the energy truce is supposed to last a week, but the Kremlin says it will end on Sunday. Russia has since switched its focus, and continued attacks on other key infrastructure.
00:00 Russia, Ukraine hold energy attacks during cold spell 02:18 Konstantin Eggert, DW Russia Analyst 03:25 Marianna Fakhurdinova, Center for European Policy Analysis
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Nazi documents withdrawn from German auction passed to Poland’s Auschwitz-Birkenau Foundation
item.author: Daniel Tilles
item.content:encoded: Keep our news free from ads and paywalls by making a donation to support our work!Notes from Poland is run by a small editorial team and is published by an independent, non-profit foundation that is funded through donations from our readers. We cannot do what we do without your support.Historical documents relating to the Holocaust and other Nazi-German crimes that were withdrawn from auction in Germany last year after international criticism and intervention by the Polish government have now been handed over to the Auschwitz-Birkenau Foundation in Poland.On Friday, the foundation announced that 433 documents had been placed under its care during a handover at the state parliament of North Rhine-Westphalia, which acquired the items after the auction was cancelled.The documents pertain to a variety of Nazi-German crimes committed during World War Two, including in ghettos and concentration camps in occupied Poland. A large section of the files relates to Auschwitz itself.Piotr Cywiński, director of the Auschwitz Museum, said that the documents would now be “transferred to appropriate institutions and memorial sites, where they will be incorporated into archival resources, preserved, academically studied…[and] used in educational work”.André Kuper, the president of the North Rhine-Westphalia state parliament, said they were “grateful that we have found a solution to transfer this collection of documents to the archives of the relevant memorial sites”.“Preserving memory in archives and museums protects the dignity of the victims and serves to further research and education about the inhumane Nazi persecution and extermination process,” he added.When the Felzmann auction house in the city of Neuss announced last November that it would be auctioning the items, which came from a private collection, it caused outrage and prompted intervention from Poland’s foreign minister, who called the planned sale “a scandal that must be prevented”.The items for sale included Stars of David worn by Jewish camp and ghetto prisoners, documents relating to forced sterilisations, records of companies forcibly sold to the Germans, and Nazi propaganda material.One letter from a prisoner at Auschwitz to a recipient in the city of Kraków – both of which were in occupied Poland – had a starting price of €500. A collection of postcards sent between the parents and children of a Jewish family in Poland was listed at €12,000.The auction was quickly cancelled and Poland’s culture minister said she would “unequivocally demand the return of these objects to Poland”.German newspaper reports that the owner of the auction house, Reinhard Fischer, had cooperated in helping the state of North Rhine-Westphalia purchase the collection. He said that he was happy “that these important documents are going to the right place and into the right hands”.According to the newspaper, the state’s purchase of the material was financed through a donation from a foundation dedicated to remembrance. The details of the arrangement are confidential, but “it is understood that a low six-figure sum was paid”.Previously, some other items from the collection were sold to the Israeli Yad Ezer L’Haver foundation, reports Notes from Poland is run by a small editorial team and published by an independent, non-profit foundation that is funded through donations from our readers. We cannot do what we do without your support.Daniel Tilles is editor-in-chief of . He has written on Polish affairs for a wide range of publications, including , , and .

item.title: US Justice Department releases more Epstein files | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
The US Department of Justice has released 3 million more pages of documents from the Epstein files, including thousands of videos and images, with references to multiple public figures including Donald Trump. US authorities are hoping to draw a line under the affair, but survivors of Epstein's crimes say it's not over yet.
00:00 US Justice Department releases more Epstein files 02:31 Mark Shanahan, Professor of Political Engagement, University of Surrey

item.title: Is the US Moving Toward State Capitalism?
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration is taking direct ownership stakes in private sector companies, from chipmaker Intel to rare earths producer MP Materials, reshaping the relationship between government and markets. Steven Rattner of Willett Advisors and Sarah Bauerle Danzman of the Atlantic Council weigh the merits of national security to justify the government picking winners and losers, and what it means for competition, innovation, and trust in free markets. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets
Like this video? Subscribe and turn on notifications so you don't miss any videos from Bloomberg Markets & Finance: https://tinyurl.com/ysu5b8a9 Visit http://www.bloomberg.com for business news & analysis, up-to-the-minute market data, features, profiles and more.

item.title: 29 people killed in Gaza by Israeli strikes, hospital sources say as ceasefire inches forward
item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:
29 people killed in Gaza by Israeli strikes, hospital sources say as ceasefire inches forward
The strikes hit locations in northern and southern Gaza, including an apartment building and a police station in Gaza City and a tent in Khan Younis, officials at hospitals that received the bodies said.

item.title: Lithuania proposes Europe’s first cross-border economic zone to Poland
item.author: Daniel Tilles
item.content:encoded: Keep our news free from ads and paywalls by making a donation to support our work!Notes from Poland is run by a small editorial team and is published by an independent, non-profit foundation that is funded through donations from our readers. We cannot do what we do without your support.Lithuania has proposed to Poland that they create Europe’s first cross-border economic zone. It says that the project, which would be located in the strategically important Suwałki Gap, would focus on attracting the defence and technology industries.Lithuania’s president, Gitanas Nausėda, raised the idea last week during a visit to Warsaw to meet his Polish counterpart, Karol Nawrocki.Lithuanian economy and innovation minister Edvinas Grikšas told broadcaster Žinių on Thursday that the idea for the economic zone had been “received positively by both sides”, which were now analysing whether and how it could be implemented.“This could be a breakthrough,” said Grikšas. “There is no such cross-border special economic zone operating in Europe. The only one [in the world] that is operating, to my knowledge, is in Singapore and Malaysia.”Grikšas said that one of his deputy ministers, Paulius Petrauskas, was travelling to Singapore to learn more about the special economic zone that it recently established with Malaysia.“It is interesting to see how they approached this issue, how it works in practice, and how they reconcile the legal issues of the two countries, for example in matters of taxation and profit sharing,” said Petrauskas, quoted by broadcaster ZW.Petrauskas said that the planned Polish-Lithuanian economic zone could accommodate both firms from the traditional defence industry and those in the technology sector that contribute to arms manufacturing.Lithuania has proposed locating the zone in the Lazdijai district, which is on the opposite side of the border from the Polish town of Suwałki.The entire Polish-Lithuanian border sits between Belarus and the Russian exclave of Kaliningrad, making it a strategic chokepoint in a potential conflict.The mayor of Suwałki, Czesław Renkiewicz, told the Polish Press Agency (PAP) that Lithuania’s proposal is a “good and interesting idea”, which could help make the region more attractive to investors who have been deterred by the “bad PR” it has had due to potential security threats.“In addition to the typical tax reliefs available in economic zones, other financial instruments should be launched for investors, such as government grants for companies investing in such a zone,” he suggested.During his visit to Warsaw, Nausėda also called for Poland and Lithuania to establish a joint military training ground in the same area.“This would be a unique solution in the NATO context, a joint training and exercise ground intended to protect the alliance’s eastern flank,” said the Lithuanian president, quoted by PAP.Lithuania and Poland enjoy close historical ties. Between the 16th and 18th centuries, they formed the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at its peak was one of the largest and most important states in Europe.Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022 has pushed the two countries towards closer cooperation, including holding joint military exercises in the Suwałki Gap. Notes from Poland is run by a small editorial team and published by an independent, non-profit foundation that is funded through donations from our readers. We cannot do what we do without your support.Daniel Tilles is editor-in-chief of . He has written on Polish affairs for a wide range of publications, including , , and .

item.title: Gardening for life: Ildikó Enyedi’s new film Silent Friend examines ties between people and plants
item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:
Gardening for life: Ildikó Enyedi’s new film Silent Friend examines ties between people and plants
Already a hit at several international film festivals, Silent Friend is an emotional and humorous tale of tales set in a German garden centre.
READ MORE : http://www.euronews.com/2026/01/31/gardening-for-life-ildiko-enyedis-new-film-silent-friend-examines-ties-between-people-and-
Spark your senses, wake your wonder. Euronews Culture seeks to show creativity in action and inspire our audience to explore the world through the five senses. Start your journey through the best of Europe's arts, gastronomy, traditions and high-end craftsmanship.
#Cinema

item.title: How did 'IShowSpeed' boost content creation on the continent? | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Twenty African countries in just four weeks: From the kick-off in Cape Town, American streamer and YouTuber IShowSpeed certainly delivered what his name promises: Zapping from Algeria to Zimbabwe, some of his destinations were already on the world's agenda - but then Benin! Who knew?
00:00 YouTuber IShowSpeed inspires content creators in Nigeria 04:32 Sharon Machiri, Travel journalist and content creator talks to DW

item.title: Public safety or power play: why were thousands of waterfront homes destroyed in Lagos? | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Thousands of people in Makoko say they've lost their livelihoods or the roof over their heads. But the government contends their homes were illegal and unsafe. We hear from an urban planner who says water and electricity can be a fatal mix.

item.title: Wall Street Week | Trump Picks Warsh, US State Capitalism, SNAP Cuts, Business of Youth Sports
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
This week, President Trump announces he intends to nominate Kevin Warsh as the next Fed Chair. Then, how government ownership is changing competition, capital allocation, and the future of US markets. Plus, will cutting SNAP destabilize the grocery, farming, and food-bank economies? Later, how big money is transforming youth sports, driving up costs and changing how families chase athletic dreams.
Chapters: 00:00:00 - Trump picks Warsh 00:11:16 - US state capitalism 00:23:51 - SNAP cut implications 00:35:57 - Youth sports & private equity 00:47:31 - How One Food Bank Braces for SNAP Cuts -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: Rebel group M23 says hundreds are dead after landslides bury mine in DR Congo | DW New
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Massive landslides in the Democratic Republic of the Congo have collapsed a militia-held mine, burying workers beneath a hillside. The exact number of casualties is unclear, but rebel officials say at least 200 have been killed. Many bodies remain buried in the mud, while those injured are being treated in nearby health facilities. Witnesses say the hillside collapsed after heavy rain. The mine produces coltan, used in the manufacturing of electronics.
00:00 Hundreds dead as landslide buries mine in DR Congo 00:31 DW correspondent Sella Oneko reports
#drcongo #m23 #rwanda
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Venezuela announces a general amnesty for political prisoners incarcerated since 1999
item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:
Venezuela announces a general amnesty for political prisoners incarcerated since 1999
Delcy Rodríguez has proposed an amnesty law that would cover political prisoners detained during the entire Chavista period. The measure excludes cases of murder, drug trafficking and human rights violations. Families are waiting cautiously.
READ MORE : http://www.euronews.com/2026/01/31/venezuela-announces-a-general-amnesty-for-political-prisoners-incarcerated-since-1999
Subscribe to our channel: https://www.youtube.com/c/euronews?sub_confirmation=1 Watch our LIVE here: https://www.youtube.com/c/euronews/live

item.title: Saudi defense deals could change Middle East security | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
From new defense deals to talks on a military bloc with Egypt and Somalia, Saudi Arabia is expanding its reach along one of the world’s most strategic waterways — the Red Sea. A defense deal with Pakistan and possibly Turkey could further boost Riyadh’s influence. Here's what’s driving this shift and why it matters far beyond the Middle East.
#dwnewsshorts For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Historic winter storm kills 10 across the United States as disruptions persist
item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:
Historic winter storm kills 10 across the United States as disruptions persist
A colossal winter storm that hit the United States on Friday is causing severe disruption and traffic chaos across the country.
READ MORE : http://www.euronews.com/2026/01/26/historic-winter-storm-kills-10-across-the-united-states-as-disruptions-persist

item.title: Where does the funding for ICE come from? #politics #shorts
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
The Big Beautiful Bill allotted more than $170 billion to immigration enforcement — a sum which surpasses the annual military spending of every country on Earth, except for China and the US.
Fola Akinnibi joins Stacey Vanek Smith and Max Chafkin on the Everybody's Business podcast to discuss the costs of ICE and of hunting down and detaining undocumented workers https://link.podtrac.com/ujb7ns2p
Title: A deep dive into the money funding ICE

item.title: Iran capable of ‘asymmetric war and creating real destruction’, despite lacking warships | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Iranian President Masoud ⁠Pezeshkian has accused the US, Europe, and Israel of stirring tensions that quote "provoked" recent protests across Iran. His accusations come as US President Donald Trump pushes Iran to negotiate a deal on its nuclear and missile programmes - or else face US military action. Sara Bazoobandi at the Institute for Security Policy at Kiel University in Germany talks to DW News
#trump #iranprotests #irannews
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: What you don't know about Greenland's minerals | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Rare earths, uranium, gold — and big expectations. Here is the real story behind Greenland's mineral potential and the consequences for the environment and local communities.
#trump #rareearthminerals #greenland

item.title: ‘Macron effect’ boosts French eyewear designer
item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:
‘Macron effect’ boosts French eyewear designer
In the Henry Jullien workshop, machines have been running at full speed since French President Macron appeared in public wearing the company's sunglasses. Founded in 1921 and specialising in high-end "made in France" eyewear, the company has seen its sales skyrocket in just a few days.
READ MORE : http://www.euronews.com/2026/01/31/macron-effect-boosts-french-eyewear-designer
Spark your senses, wake your wonder. Euronews Culture seeks to show creativity in action and inspire our audience to explore the world through the five senses. Start your journey through the best of Europe's arts, gastronomy, traditions and high-end craftsmanship.
#CultureNews

item.title: Saudi Arabia Looks to Wealthy Families in Quest for Cash
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Saudi Arabia is turning to the kingdom's wealthiest families to ease pressure on public finances and fund the next phase of Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman's economic overhaul. Bloomberg's Christine Burke explains. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: Amazon’s Melania Trump film opens in cinemas worldwide, with lackluster ticket sales | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Melania Trump is marking her first year back as U-S first lady with the global release of a documentary she produced. The film - simply titled 'Melania' - follows the 20 days leading up to Donald Trump’s return to the White House for his second term. It’s opening in more than 1500 theatres in the U-S and abroad. But reception has been lukewarm. Some countries like South Africa have refused to screen it at all, amid strained relations with the United States. DW Reporter Shristi Pal has more.
#melaniatrump #trump
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Africa’s Gambling Boom: Investment, Risk and Regulation
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Sports wagering is booming in Africa. Fueled by a young population, and access to latest technology, legal gambling revenues across the continent have nearly tripled in the past decade, turning it into a multi-billion-dollar market that global operators can no longer ignore. Bloomberg's Olatomiwa Babalola and Senior Africa reporter Loni Prinsloo explain. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: Why Trump's Fed chair pick Warsh will try to slash interest rates if confirmed by Senate | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
US President Donald Trump has announced Kevin Warsh as his choice to head the US central bank, known as the Federal Reserve or Fed. He'll replace Jerome Powell, who steps down in May. Warsh is a Republican and a former Fed governor. The announcement sent the US dollar soaring and the price of gold and silver plunging, apparently relieving concerns about the Fed's independence. Warsh is seen as favouring lower borrowing costs, in line with Trump's wishes. But Trump denies asking Warsh directly to cut interest rates.
Chapter Breakdown 0:00 Who is Kevin Warsh, Trump's new pick to head the Fed? 0:52 Allie Kelly, reporter at Business Insider, on why Trump is interfering at the Fed
#federalreserve #usfed #kevinwarsh
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: 'Rein in' ICE: How serious is the bipartisan effort to put guardrails on immigration enforcement?
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Republicans are starting to shift their tone, criticizing Trump's anti-immigration campaign, following the killings Renee Good and Alex Pretti. A number of Republican mayors condemn the hardline enforcement tactics used, saying their cities are no longer safe. DW Correspondent Misha Komadovsky went to the Trump stronghold of Hanover County, Virginia, where there's opposition to plans for a new ICE facility.
#ice #kristinoem #dhsfunding
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Trump says he asked Putin to temporarily cease attacks on Ukraine's infrastructure due to cold spell
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Russia has agreed to pause strikes on the Ukrainian capital Kyiv until Sunday. This comes after US President Trump asked Russia's President Putin to halt attacks on Ukraine's energy grid for a week because of the extreme winter cold.
DW speaks to Inna Sovsun, Ukrainian MP in Kyiv, on how Ukraine's energy and infrastructure has been targeted by Russia.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: US students, businesses, and Hollywood lead 'national shutdown' strike against ICE and CBP | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
US businesses and schools went on strike on Friday to protest federal immigration tactics. The strikes are meant to force a 'national shutdown' to put pressure on the Trump administration to pull immigration enforcement ages from US cities.
Chapter Breakdown 0:00 Strikes and protests held across the US 2:31 Rebecca Winter, Spokesperson for grassroots movement 50501, on the movement to abolish ICE #ice #iceminneapolis #nationalshutdown
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Senate Votes on Government Funding Bill | Balance of Power 01/30/2026
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
"Balance of Power: Late Edition" focuses on the intersection of politics and global business. On today's show, Senator Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) shares her thoughts on President Trump nominating Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve Chair to replace Jerome Powell. Patrick McHenry, former Representative (R-NC) on what to expect after the Senate votes on government funding.
Chapters: 00:00:00 - Balance of Power: Late Edition Begins 00:00:41 - Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) on Gov. Funding Bill, DHS Bill Latest, Reaction to Kevin Warsh as Pres. Trump Fed Chair Pick 00:07:36 - Michael McKee on Kevin Warsh’s Policy Views, Fed. Governor Miran on Staying at the Fed, Trump to Nominate Brett Matsumoto for BLS Head 00:12:17 - Power Moves: Top Financial Stories of the Day 00:15:32 - Fmr. Rep. Patrick McHenry (R-NC) on Possible Govt. Shutdown, What to Expect Next In House, Thoughts on Kevin Warsh 00:26:58 - Kate Sullivan on What Was the Process Behind Trump Picking Kevin Warsh as Fed Chair Nominee 00:30:33 - Enda Curran Previews What Policies From a Potential Fed Chair Kevin Warsh Could Look Like 00:36:10 - House to Vote Monday Evening on Senate Funding Deal if Passed 00:37:21 - Power Brief: Top Stories Watching Tonight 00:38:37 - Political Panel React to Significance of White House Negotiating on DHS Funding, Reaction to Trump Taps Kevin Warsh as Fed Chair 00:47:19 - Senate Voting on Government Funding Bill Latest -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: Patrick McHenry on Govt. Funding Vote, Kevin Warsh
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Patrick McHenry, former Representative (R) North Carolina, discusses what he expects will happen in the House of Representatives early next week as the Senate votes on the government funding bill. McHenry also shares his thoughts on President Trump picking Kevin Warsh as his Federal Reserve Chair nominee. He speaks with Joe Mathieu on the late edition of Bloomberg's "Balance of Power." -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: Which economic targets will China pursue in its next five-year plan — and are they bold enough?
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
China's Communist Party leadership will soon release its Five-Year Plan which will run until 2030. The plan is an economic blueprint for China's longterm development. Analysts say it will likely set out ambitious targets for technological advances and high-end manufacturing.
DW's China analyst Clifford Coonan explains what China hopes to achieve with its next five-year plan.
#china #chinafiveyearplan #xi
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
